title: Operationally Admissible Post-Quantum Correlations from a Standard Quantum Walk
abstract: It is shown that a standard one-dimensional coined discrete-time quantum walk can generate operationally admissible post-quantum correlations in a coin-position Bell scenario, without any modification of its unitary nearest-neighbor dynamics. Post-quantum features enter exclusively through an extended operational preparation of the coin, described by a complementarity-violating Hermitian trace-one operator, while physical consistency is enforced solely at the level of observable statistics via admissibility and no-signaling. The extended preparation admits an experimental emulation through a two-component quasiprobability reconstruction over physical coin states, at the price of an increased sampling overhead. The walk-generated coin-position entanglement can support CHSH values exceeding Tsirelson's bound, even though the walk dynamics remains fully standard. We also show that physically natural coarse-grained position measurements can render such post-quantum correlations operationally inaccessible, strongly suppressing observable Bell violations. The purpose here is to contrast the separation between the existence of post-quantum behavior and its accessibility under realistic measurement constraints.
summary: This paper demonstrates that a standard quantum walk can generate correlations that exceed the Tsirelson bound if the initial coin state is prepared using an extended, non-positive framework. However, it reveals that these post-quantum features are practically hidden from observers using realistic, coarse-grained position measurements as the system evolves. The work highlights a fundamental gap between the existence of post-quantum resources and their operational accessibility.
main_problem: Investigating whether post-quantum correlations (violating the Tsirelson bound) can be operationally accessed within a standard quantum walk framework and distinguishing between their existence in a state versus their accessibility under realistic measurement constraints.
main_result: The study finds that while standard quantum walk dynamics can support post-quantum correlations via an extended coin preparation, these correlations are strongly suppressed and become inaccessible under physically natural, coarse-grained position measurements as the walk time increases.
method: The authors use a numerical approach involving randomized searches over preparation and measurement directions, comparing optimized Schmidt-aligned measurements against coarse-grained position binning, and employing a quasiprobability reconstruction to emulate non-positive coin preparations.
model_or_system: A one-dimensional, discrete-time, coined quantum walk on a lattice (Z) consisting of a two-level coin and a high-dimensional walker subspace, utilizing standard Hadamard coin and nearest-neighbor shift dynamics.
key_observables: CHSH Bell parameter (S), joint probabilities p(a, b|i, j), and Schmidt coefficients.
important_parameters: Bloch vector magnitude of the coin preparation (||r||), walk time (T), and position binning threshold ratio (x0/T).
main_assumptions: The framework assumes that physical consistency can be maintained via admissibility (non-negative probabilities) and no-signaling at the level of observed statistics, even if the underlying state preparation is non-positive.
figures_summary: Figure 1 summarizes the separation between existence and accessibility; Figure 2 shows CHSH values exceeding Tsirelson's bound for Schmidt-aligned measurements; Figure 3 shows the admissibility margin; Figure 6 and 7 demonstrate the suppression of Bell violations in coarse-grained measurements as walk time increases.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the problem of post-quantum correlations, defines the extended preparation framework and quantum walk model, presents a benchmark using optimized measurements to show existence, then explores the accessibility gap using coarse-grained measurements, and concludes by discussing the scaling effects and experimental implications.
why_interesting: It provides a controlled theoretical platform to study the boundary between quantum and post-quantum regimes and highlights how measurement resolution and structure can fundamentally limit the observation of non-classical resources in many-body-like dynamics.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: FIG. 1. Schematic illustration of the separation between the existence and operational accessibility of post-quantum correlations in a standard coined quantum walk. Left: a fully standard, unitary nearest-neighbor quantum walk on a one-dimensional lattice, with a two-level coin controlling the conditional shift. Center: entanglement between the coin (two-dimensional Hilbert space) and the walker position generates an effective low-dimensional Schmidt subspace within the large position Hilbert space. Right: two measurement regimes. Schmidt-aligned observables access this subspace and reveal admissible post-quantum CHSH violations, while coarse-grained position measurements fail to resolve...

FIG. 2. CHSH value |S| versus extended Bloch magnitude ∥r∥for the fixed Schmidt-aligned benchmark at T = 60. Markers show the discrete maximizers obtained from the directional scan at each sampled ∥r∥, and the solid curve is a guide to the eye connecting these points. Horizontal reference lines indicate the classical CHSH bound |S| = 2 (dashed), Tsirelson’s bound |S| = 2 √

FIG. 3. Minimum joint probability mina,b,i,j p(a, b|i, j) for the Schmidt-aligned benchmark, demonstrating admissibility of the post-quantum violations. The dashed horizontal line indicates the boundary min p = 0.

FIG. 4. Final-time walker position distribution P(x) at T = 60 for the maximal-violation Schmidt-aligned witness at ∥r∥= 1.45, with |S| = 3.0810. The distribution remains confined to |x| ≤T and exhibits the familiar interference-broadened profile of a standard Hadamard coined quantum walk.

FIG. 5. Final-time walker position distribution P(x) at T = 60 for the coarse-grained witness of Table I. The distribution remains confined to |x| ≤T and shows the familiar interference-broadened profile of a standard Hadamard coined quantum walk.

Spin-momentum entanglement.note: The paper directly studies the entanglement between the internal coin state (spin) and the external position/momentum states of the walker, specifically regarding how this entanglement affects the accessibility of post-quantum correlations.
